sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the current state and try to understand the range of section widths
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current state:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        print(f"  white rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} width={max(cols)-min(cols)+1}")

# Try clicking blue block 1 multiple times to see if upper width can exceed 56
print("\nTrying to increase upper width beyond 56:")
for i in range(5):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 1}])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    widths = []
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'W':
            b = n['boundary']
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            widths.append(max(cols)-min(cols)+1)
    widths.sort(reverse=True)
    print(f"  After click {i+1}: widths = {widths}")
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed', False):
        print("  Level completed!")
        break
    if last_action_result.get('no_impact', False):
        print("  No impact (width didn't change)")
        break

# Now try clicking blue block 2 multiple times to see the minimum upper width
print("\nTrying to decrease upper width:")
for i in range(10):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 1}])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    widths = []
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'W':
            b = n['boundary']
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            widths.append(max(cols)-min(cols)+1)
    widths.sort(reverse=True)
    print(f"  After click {i+1}: widths = {widths}")
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed', False):
        print("  Level completed!")
        break
    if last_action_result.get('no_impact', False):
        print("  No impact (width didn't change)")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
